patch.description:
Updates session state without running the agent.

Args:
    app_name: The name of the application.
    user_id: The ID of the user.
    session_id: The ID of the session to update.
    req: The patch request containing state changes.

Returns:
    The updated session.

Raises:
    HTTPException: If the session is not found.

Adds all events from a given session to the memory service.

AgentDetails.title: AgentDetails
AgentDetails.description:
Details about the individual agent in the App.

This could be a root agent or the sub-agents in the Agent Tree.

AppDetails.title: AppDetails
AppDetails.description:
Contains details about the App (the agentic system).

This structure is only a projection of the actual app. Only details
that are relevant to the Eval System are captured here.

ArtifactVersion.title: ArtifactVersion

AuthConfig-Input.title: AuthConfig
AuthConfig-Input.description:
The auth config sent by tool asking client to collect auth credentials and

adk and client will help to fill in the response

AuthConfig-Output.title: AuthConfig
AuthConfig-Output.description:
The auth config sent by tool asking client to collect auth credentials and

adk and client will help to fill in the response

AuthCredential-Input.title: AuthCredential
AuthCredential-Input.description:
Data class representing an authentication credential.

To exchange for the actual credential, please use
CredentialExchanger.exchange_credential().

Examples: API Key Auth
AuthCredential(
    auth_type=AuthCredentialTypes.API_KEY,
    api_key="1234",
)

Example: HTTP Auth
AuthCredential(
    auth_type=AuthCredentialTypes.HTTP,
    http=HttpAuth(
        scheme="basic",
        credentials=HttpCredentials(username="user", password="password"),
    ),
)

Example: OAuth2 Bearer Token in HTTP Header
AuthCredential(
    auth_type=AuthCredentialTypes.HTTP,
    http=HttpAuth(
        scheme="bearer",
        credentials=HttpCredentials(token="eyAkaknabna...."),
    ),
)

Example: OAuth2 Auth with Authorization Code Flow
AuthCredential(
    auth_type=AuthCredentialTypes.OAUTH2,
    oauth2=OAuth2Auth(
        client_id="1234",
        client_secret="secret",
    ),
)

Example: OpenID Connect Auth
AuthCredential(
    auth_type=AuthCredentialTypes.OPEN_ID_CONNECT,
    oauth2=OAuth2Auth(
        client_id="1234",
        client_secret="secret",
        redirect_uri="https://example.com",
        scopes=["scope1", "scope2"],
    ),
)

Example: Auth with resource reference
AuthCredential(
    auth_type=AuthCredentialTypes.API_KEY,
    resource_ref="projects/1234/locations/us-central1/resources/resource1",
)

AuthCredential-Output.title: AuthCredential
AuthCredential-Output.description:
Data class representing an authentication credential.

To exchange for the actual credential, please use
CredentialExchanger.exchange_credential().

Examples: API Key Auth
AuthCredential(
    auth_type=AuthCredentialTypes.API_KEY,
    api_key="1234",
)

Example: HTTP Auth
AuthCredential(
    auth_type=AuthCredentialTypes.HTTP,
    http=HttpAuth(
        scheme="basic",
        credentials=HttpCredentials(username="user", password="password"),
    ),
)

Example: OAuth2 Bearer Token in HTTP Header
AuthCredential(
    auth_type=AuthCredentialTypes.HTTP,
    http=HttpAuth(
        scheme="bearer",
        credentials=HttpCredentials(token="eyAkaknabna...."),
    ),
)

Example: OAuth2 Auth with Authorization Code Flow
AuthCredential(
    auth_type=AuthCredentialTypes.OAUTH2,
    oauth2=OAuth2Auth(
        client_id="1234",
        client_secret="secret",
    ),
)

Example: OpenID Connect Auth
AuthCredential(
    auth_type=AuthCredentialTypes.OPEN_ID_CONNECT,
    oauth2=OAuth2Auth(
        client_id="1234",
        client_secret="secret",
        redirect_uri="https://example.com",
        scopes=["scope1", "scope2"],
    ),
)

Example: Auth with resource reference
AuthCredential(
    auth_type=AuthCredentialTypes.API_KEY,
    resource_ref="projects/1234/locations/us-central1/resources/resource1",
)

AuthCredentialTypes.title: AuthCredentialTypes
AuthCredentialTypes.description: Represents the type of authentication credential.

data.description: Required. The raw bytes of the data.

displayName.title: Displayname
displayName.description: Optional. The display name of the blob. Used to provide a label or filename to distinguish blobs. This field is only returned in `PromptMessage` for prompt management. It is used in the Gemini calls only when server-side tools (`code_execution`, `google_search`, and `url_context`) are enabled. This field is not supported in Gemini API.

mimeType.title: Mimetype

A content blob.

A Blob contains data of a specific media type. It is used to represent images,
audio, and video.

cache_name.title: Cache Name
cache_name.description: Full resource name of the cached content (None if no active cache)

expire_time.title: Expire Time
expire_time.description: Unix timestamp when cache expires (None if no active cache)

fingerprint.title: Fingerprint
fingerprint.description: Hash of cacheable contents used to detect changes

invocations_used.title: Invocations Used
invocations_used.description: Number of invocations this cache has been used for (None if no active cache)

contents_count.title: Contents Count
contents_count.description: Number of contents (cached contents when active cache exists, total contents in request when no active cache)

created_at.title: Created At
created_at.description: Unix timestamp when cache was created (None if no active cache)

CacheMetadata.title: CacheMetadata
CacheMetadata.description:
Metadata for context cache associated with LLM responses.

This class stores cache identification, usage tracking, and lifecycle
information for a particular cache instance. It can be in two states:

1. Active cache state: cache_name is set, all fields populated
2. Fingerprint-only state: cache_name is None, only fingerprint and
   contents_count are set for prefix matching

Token counts (cached and total) are available in the LlmResponse.usage_metadata
and should be accessed from there to avoid duplication.

Attributes:
    cache_name: The full resource name of the cached content (e.g.,
        'projects/123/locations/us-central1/cachedContents/456').
        None when no active cache exists (fingerprint-only state).
    expire_time: Unix timestamp when the cache expires. None when no
        active cache exists.
    fingerprint: Hash of cacheable contents (instruction + tools + contents).
        Always present for prefix matching.
    invocations_used: Number of invocations this cache has been used for.
        None when no active cache exists.
    contents_count: Number of contents. When active cache exists, this is
        the count of cached contents. When no active cache exists, this is
        the total count of contents in the request.
    created_at: Unix timestamp when the cache was created. None when
        no active cache exists.

EvalResult.title: EvalResult
EvalResult.description:
This class has no field intentionally.

The goal here is to just give a new name to the class to align with the API
endpoint.

Event-Input.title: Event
Event-Input.description:
Represents an event in a conversation between agents and users.

It is used to store the content of the conversation, as well as the actions
taken by the agents like function calls, etc.

Event-Output.title: Event
Event-Output.description:
Represents an event in a conversation between agents and users.

It is used to store the content of the conversation, as well as the actions
taken by the agents like function calls, etc.

EventActions-Input.title: EventActions
EventActions-Input.description: Represents the actions attached to an event.

EventActions-Output.title: EventActions
EventActions-Output.description: Represents the actions attached to an event.

code.title: Code
code.description: Required. The code to be executed.

id.title: Id
id.description: Unique identifier of the `ExecutableCode` part. The server returns the `CodeExecutionResult` with the matching `id`.

ExecutableCode.title: ExecutableCode
ExecutableCode.description:
Model-generated code executed server-side, results returned to the model.

Only generated when using the `CodeExecution` tool, in which the code will
be automatically executed, and a corresponding `CodeExecutionResult` will
also be generated.

displayName.title: Displayname
displayName.description: Optional. The display name of the file. Used to provide a label or filename to distinguish files. This field is only returned in `PromptMessage` for prompt management. It is used in the Gemini calls only when server side tools (`code_execution`, `google_search`, and `url_context`) are enabled. This field is not supported in Gemini API.

fileUri.title: Fileuri
fileUri.description: Required. The URI of the file in Google Cloud Storage.

mimeType.title: Mimetype
mimeType.description: Required. The IANA standard MIME type of the source data.

FileData.title: FileData
FileData.description:
URI-based data.

A FileData message contains a URI pointing to data of a specific media type.
It is used to represent images, audio, and video stored in Google Cloud
Storage.

FinishReason.title: FinishReason
FinishReason.description:
Output only. The reason why the model stopped generating tokens.

If empty, the model has not stopped generating the tokens.

id.title: Id
id.description:
The unique id of the function call. If populated, the client to execute the
   `function_call` and return the response with the matching `id`.

args.title: Args
args.description: Optional. The function parameters and values in JSON object format. See [FunctionDeclaration.parameters] for parameter details.

name.title: Name
name.description: Optional. The name of the function to call. Matches [FunctionDeclaration.name].

partialArgs.title: Partialargs
partialArgs.description: Optional. The partial argument value of the function call. If provided, represents the arguments/fields that are streamed incrementally. This field is not supported in Gemini API.

willContinue.title: Willcontinue
willContinue.description: Optional. Whether this is the last part of the FunctionCall. If true, another partial message for the current FunctionCall is expected to follow. This field is not supported in Gemini API.

willContinue.title: Willcontinue
willContinue.description: Signals that function call continues, and more responses will be returned, turning the function call into a generator. Is only applicable to NON_BLOCKING function calls (see FunctionDeclaration.behavior for details), ignored otherwise. If false, the default, future responses will not be considered. Is only applicable to NON_BLOCKING function calls, is ignored otherwise. If set to false, future responses will not be considered. It is allowed to return empty `response` with `will_continue=False` to signal that the function call is finished.

scheduling.description: Specifies how the response should be scheduled in the conversation. Only applicable to NON_BLOCKING function calls, is ignored otherwise. Defaults to WHEN_IDLE.

parts.title: Parts
parts.description:
List of parts that constitute a function response. Each part may
      have a different IANA MIME type.

id.title: Id
id.description: Optional. The id of the function call this response is for. Populated by the client to match the corresponding function call `id`.

name.title: Name
name.description: Required. The name of the function to call. Matches [FunctionDeclaration.name] and [FunctionCall.name].

response.title: Response
response.description: Required. The function response in JSON object format. Use "output" key to specify function output and "error" key to specify error details (if any). If "output" and "error" keys are not specified, then whole "response" is treated as function output.

willContinue.title: Willcontinue
willContinue.description: Signals that function call continues, and more responses will be returned, turning the function call into a generator. Is only applicable to NON_BLOCKING function calls (see FunctionDeclaration.behavior for details), ignored otherwise. If false, the default, future responses will not be considered. Is only applicable to NON_BLOCKING function calls, is ignored otherwise. If set to false, future responses will not be considered. It is allowed to return empty `response` with `will_continue=False` to signal that the function call is finished.

scheduling.description: Specifies how the response should be scheduled in the conversation. Only applicable to NON_BLOCKING function calls, is ignored otherwise. Defaults to WHEN_IDLE.

parts.title: Parts
parts.description:
List of parts that constitute a function response. Each part may
      have a different IANA MIME type.

id.title: Id
id.description: Optional. The id of the function call this response is for. Populated by the client to match the corresponding function call `id`.

name.title: Name
name.description: Required. The name of the function to call. Matches [FunctionDeclaration.name] and [FunctionCall.name].

response.title: Response
response.description: Required. The function response in JSON object format. Use "output" key to specify function output and "error" key to specify error details (if any). If "output" and "error" keys are not specified, then whole "response" is treated as function output.

mimeType.title: Mimetype
mimeType.description: Required. The IANA standard MIME type of the source data.

displayName.title: Displayname
displayName.description:
Optional. Display name of the blob.
      Used to provide a label or filename to distinguish blobs.

FunctionResponseBlob.title: FunctionResponseBlob
FunctionResponseBlob.description:
Raw media bytes for function response.

Text should not be sent as raw bytes, use the FunctionResponse.response
field.

mimeType.title: Mimetype
mimeType.description: Required. The IANA standard MIME type of the source data.

displayName.title: Displayname
displayName.description:
Optional. Display name of the file.
      Used to provide a label or filename to distinguish files.

FunctionResponseFileData.title: FunctionResponseFileData
FunctionResponseFileData.description: URI based data for function response.

FunctionResponsePart.title: FunctionResponsePart
FunctionResponsePart.description:
A datatype containing media that is part of a `FunctionResponse` message.

A `FunctionResponsePart` consists of data which has an associated datatype. A
`FunctionResponsePart` can only contain one of the accepted types in
`FunctionResponsePart.data`.

A `FunctionResponsePart` must have a fixed IANA MIME type identifying the
type and subtype of the media if the `inline_data` field is filled with raw
bytes.

FunctionResponseScheduling.title: FunctionResponseScheduling
FunctionResponseScheduling.description: Specifies how the response should be scheduled in the conversation.

cacheTokensDetails.title: Cachetokensdetails
cacheTokensDetails.description: Output only. A detailed breakdown of the token count for each modality in the cached content.

cachedContentTokenCount.title: Cachedcontenttokencount
cachedContentTokenCount.description: Output only. The number of tokens in the cached content that was used for this request.

candidatesTokenCount.title: Candidatestokencount
candidatesTokenCount.description: The total number of tokens in the generated candidates.

candidatesTokensDetails.title: Candidatestokensdetails
candidatesTokensDetails.description: Output only. A detailed breakdown of the token count for each modality in the generated candidates.

promptTokenCount.title: Prompttokencount
promptTokenCount.description: The total number of tokens in the prompt. This includes any text, images, or other media provided in the request. When `cached_content` is set, this also includes the number of tokens in the cached content.

promptTokensDetails.title: Prompttokensdetails
promptTokensDetails.description: Output only. A detailed breakdown of the token count for each modality in the prompt.

thoughtsTokenCount.title: Thoughtstokencount
thoughtsTokenCount.description: Output only. The number of tokens that were part of the model's generated "thoughts" output, if applicable.

toolUsePromptTokenCount.title: Tooluseprompttokencount
toolUsePromptTokenCount.description: Output only. The number of tokens in the results from tool executions, which are provided back to the model as input, if applicable.

toolUsePromptTokensDetails.title: Tooluseprompttokensdetails
toolUsePromptTokensDetails.description: Output only. A detailed breakdown by modality of the token counts from the results of tool executions, which are provided back to the model as input.

totalTokenCount.title: Totaltokencount
totalTokenCount.description: The total number of tokens for the entire request. This is the sum of `prompt_token_count`, `candidates_token_count`, `tool_use_prompt_token_count`, and `thoughts_token_count`.

trafficType.description: Output only. The traffic type for this request.

GenerateContentResponseUsageMetadata-Inp.title: GenerateContentResponseUsageMetadata
GenerateContentResponseUsageMetadata-Inp.description:
Usage metadata about the content generation request and response.

This message provides a detailed breakdown of token usage and other relevant
metrics. This data type is not supported in Gemini API.

cacheTokensDetails.description: Output only. A detailed breakdown of the token count for each modality in the cached content.

cachedContentTokenCount.title: Cachedcontenttokencount
cachedContentTokenCount.description: Output only. The number of tokens in the cached content that was used for this request.

candidatesTokenCount.title: Candidatestokencount
candidatesTokenCount.description: The total number of tokens in the generated candidates.

candidatesTokensDetails.description: Output only. A detailed breakdown of the token count for each modality in the generated candidates.

promptTokenCount.title: Prompttokencount
promptTokenCount.description: The total number of tokens in the prompt. This includes any text, images, or other media provided in the request. When `cached_content` is set, this also includes the number of tokens in the cached content.

promptTokensDetails.title: Prompttokensdetails
promptTokensDetails.description: Output only. A detailed breakdown of the token count for each modality in the prompt.

thoughtsTokenCount.title: Thoughtstokencount
thoughtsTokenCount.description: Output only. The number of tokens that were part of the model's generated "thoughts" output, if applicable.

toolUsePromptTokenCount.title: Tooluseprompttokencount
toolUsePromptTokenCount.description: Output only. The number of tokens in the results from tool executions, which are provided back to the model as input, if applicable.

toolUsePromptTokensDetails.title: Tooluseprompttokensdetails
toolUsePromptTokensDetails.description: Output only. A detailed breakdown by modality of the token counts from the results of tool executions, which are provided back to the model as input.

totalTokenCount.title: Totaltokencount
totalTokenCount.description: The total number of tokens for the entire request. This is the sum of `prompt_token_count`, `candidates_token_count`, `tool_use_prompt_token_count`, and `thoughts_token_count`.

GenerateContentResponseUsageMetadata-Out.title: GenerateContentResponseUsageMetadata
GenerateContentResponseUsageMetadata-Out.description:
Usage metadata about the content generation request and response.

This message provides a detailed breakdown of token usage and other relevant
metrics. This data type is not supported in Gemini API.

day.title: Day
day.description: Day of a month. Must be from 1 to 31 and valid for the year and month, or 0 to specify a year by itself or a year and month where the day isn't significant.

month.title: Month
month.description: Month of a year. Must be from 1 to 12, or 0 to specify a year without a month and day.

year.title: Year
year.description: Year of the date. Must be from 1 to 9999, or 0 to specify a date without a year.

GoogleTypeDate.title: GoogleTypeDate
GoogleTypeDate.description:
Represents a whole or partial calendar date, such as a birthday.

The time of day and time zone are either specified elsewhere or are
insignificant. The date is relative to the Gregorian Calendar. This can
represent one of the following: * A full date, with non-zero year, month, and
day values. * A month and day, with a zero year (for example, an anniversary).
* A year on its own, with a zero month and a zero day. * A year and month,
with a zero day (for example, a credit card expiration date). Related types: *
google.type.TimeOfDay * google.type.DateTime * google.protobuf.Timestamp. This
data type is not supported in Gemini API.

image.description:
A grounding chunk from an image search result. See the `Image`
      message for details.

maps.description:
A `Maps` chunk is a piece of evidence that comes from Google Maps.

      It contains information about a place, such as its name, address, and
      reviews. This is used to provide the user with rich, location-based
      information.

retrievedContext.description: A grounding chunk from a data source retrieved by a retrieval tool, such as Vertex AI Search. See the `RetrievedContext` message for details. This field is not supported in Gemini API.

web.description: A grounding chunk from a web page, typically from Google Search. See the `Web` message for details.

GroundingChunk-Input.title: GroundingChunk
GroundingChunk-Input.description:
A piece of evidence that supports a claim made by the model.

This is used to show a citation for a claim made by the model. When grounding
is enabled, the model returns a `GroundingChunk` that contains a reference to
the source of the information.

image.description:
A grounding chunk from an image search result. See the `Image`
      message for details.

maps.description:
A `Maps` chunk is a piece of evidence that comes from Google Maps.

      It contains information about a place, such as its name, address, and
      reviews. This is used to provide the user with rich, location-based
      information.

retrievedContext.description: A grounding chunk from a data source retrieved by a retrieval tool, such as Vertex AI Search. See the `RetrievedContext` message for details. This field is not supported in Gemini API.

web.description: A grounding chunk from a web page, typically from Google Search. See the `Web` message for details.

GroundingChunk-Output.title: GroundingChunk
GroundingChunk-Output.description:
A piece of evidence that supports a claim made by the model.

This is used to show a citation for a claim made by the model. When grounding
is enabled, the model returns a `GroundingChunk` that contains a reference to
the source of the information.

sourceUri.title: Sourceuri
sourceUri.description: The URI of the image search result page.

title.title: Title
title.description: The title of the image search result page.

domain.title: Domain
domain.description: The domain of the image search result page.

GroundingChunkImage.title: GroundingChunkImage
GroundingChunkImage.description:
A piece of evidence that comes from an image search result.

It contains the URI of the image search result and the URI of the image.
This is used to provide the user with a link to the source of the
information.

placeAnswerSources.description:
The sources that were used to generate the place answer.

      This includes review snippets and photos that were used to generate the
      answer, as well as URIs to flag content.

placeId.title: Placeid
placeId.description:
This Place's resource name, in `places/{place_id}` format.

      This can be used to look up the place in the Google Maps API.

text.title: Text
text.description: The text of the place answer.

GroundingChunkMaps-Input.title: GroundingChunkMaps
GroundingChunkMaps-Input.description:
A `Maps` chunk is a piece of evidence that comes from Google Maps.

It contains information about a place, such as its name, address, and reviews.
This is used to provide the user with rich, location-based information.

placeAnswerSources.description:
The sources that were used to generate the place answer.

      This includes review snippets and photos that were used to generate the
      answer, as well as URIs to flag content.

placeId.title: Placeid
placeId.description:
This Place's resource name, in `places/{place_id}` format.

      This can be used to look up the place in the Google Maps API.

text.title: Text
text.description: The text of the place answer.

GroundingChunkMaps-Output.title: GroundingChunkMaps
GroundingChunkMaps-Output.description:
A `Maps` chunk is a piece of evidence that comes from Google Maps.

It contains information about a place, such as its name, address, and reviews.
This is used to provide the user with rich, location-based information.

reviewSnippet.title: Reviewsnippet
reviewSnippet.description: Snippets of reviews that were used to generate the answer.

flagContentUri.title: Flagcontenturi
flagContentUri.description: A link where users can flag a problem with the generated answer.

reviewSnippets.title: Reviewsnippets
reviewSnippets.description: Snippets of reviews that were used to generate the answer.

GroundingChunkMapsPlaceAnswerSources-Inp.title: GroundingChunkMapsPlaceAnswerSources
GroundingChunkMapsPlaceAnswerSources-Inp.description:
The sources that were used to generate the place answer.

This includes review snippets and photos that were used to generate the
answer, as well as URIs to flag content.

reviewSnippet.title: Reviewsnippet
reviewSnippet.description: Snippets of reviews that were used to generate the answer.

flagContentUri.title: Flagcontenturi
flagContentUri.description: A link where users can flag a problem with the generated answer.

reviewSnippets.title: Reviewsnippets
reviewSnippets.description: Snippets of reviews that were used to generate the answer.

GroundingChunkMapsPlaceAnswerSources-Out.title: GroundingChunkMapsPlaceAnswerSources
GroundingChunkMapsPlaceAnswerSources-Out.description:
The sources that were used to generate the place answer.

This includes review snippets and photos that were used to generate the
answer, as well as URIs to flag content.

displayName.title: Displayname
displayName.description: Name of the author of the Photo or Review.

photoUri.title: Photouri
photoUri.description: Profile photo URI of the author of the Photo or Review.

uri.title: Uri
uri.description: URI of the author of the Photo or Review.

GroundingChunkMapsPlaceAnswerSourcesAuth.title: GroundingChunkMapsPlaceAnswerSourcesAuthorAttribution
GroundingChunkMapsPlaceAnswerSourcesAuth.description: Author attribution for a photo or review.

flagContentUri.title: Flagcontenturi
flagContentUri.description: A link where users can flag a problem with the review.

googleMapsUri.title: Googlemapsuri
googleMapsUri.description: A link to show the review on Google Maps.

relativePublishTimeDescription.title: Relativepublishtimedescription
relativePublishTimeDescription.description: A string of formatted recent time, expressing the review time relative to the current time in a form appropriate for the language and country.

review.title: Review
review.description: A reference representing this place review which may be used to look up this place review again.

reviewId.title: Reviewid
reviewId.description: Id of the review referencing the place.

documentName.title: Documentname
documentName.description: Output only. The full resource name of the referenced Vertex AI Search document. This is used to identify the specific document that was retrieved. The format is `projects/{project}/locations/{location}/collections/{collection}/dataStores/{data_store}/branches/{branch}/documents/{document}`.

ragChunk.description: Additional context for a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) retrieval result. This is populated only when the RAG retrieval tool is used.

text.title: Text
text.description: The content of the retrieved data source.

title.title: Title
title.description: The title of the retrieved data source.

uri.title: Uri
uri.description: The URI of the retrieved data source.

GroundingChunkRetrievedContext-Input.title: GroundingChunkRetrievedContext
GroundingChunkRetrievedContext-Input.description:
Context retrieved from a data source to ground the model's response.

This is used when a retrieval tool fetches information from a user-provided
corpus or a public dataset. This data type is not supported in Gemini API.

documentName.title: Documentname
documentName.description: Output only. The full resource name of the referenced Vertex AI Search document. This is used to identify the specific document that was retrieved. The format is `projects/{project}/locations/{location}/collections/{collection}/dataStores/{data_store}/branches/{branch}/documents/{document}`.

ragChunk.description: Additional context for a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) retrieval result. This is populated only when the RAG retrieval tool is used.

text.title: Text
text.description: The content of the retrieved data source.

title.title: Title
title.description: The title of the retrieved data source.

uri.title: Uri
uri.description: The URI of the retrieved data source.

GroundingChunkRetrievedContext-Output.title: GroundingChunkRetrievedContext
GroundingChunkRetrievedContext-Output.description:
Context retrieved from a data source to ground the model's response.

This is used when a retrieval tool fetches information from a user-provided
corpus or a public dataset. This data type is not supported in Gemini API.

domain.title: Domain
domain.description: The domain of the web page that contains the evidence. This can be used to filter out low-quality sources. This field is not supported in Gemini API.

title.title: Title
title.description: The title of the web page that contains the evidence.

uri.title: Uri
uri.description: The URI of the web page that contains the evidence.

GroundingChunkWeb.title: GroundingChunkWeb
GroundingChunkWeb.description:
A `Web` chunk is a piece of evidence that comes from a web page.

It contains the URI of the web page, the title of the page, and the domain of
the page. This is used to provide the user with a link to the source of the
information.

imageSearchQueries.title: Imagesearchqueries
imageSearchQueries.description:
Optional. The image search queries that were used to generate the
      content. This field is populated only when the grounding source is Google
      Search with the Image Search search_type enabled.

groundingChunks.title: Groundingchunks
groundingChunks.description:
A list of supporting references retrieved from the grounding
      source. This field is populated when the grounding source is Google
      Search, Vertex AI Search, or Google Maps.

retrievalMetadata.description: Metadata related to retrieval in the grounding flow.

searchEntryPoint.description:
Optional. Google search entry for the following-up web
      searches.

webSearchQueries.title: Websearchqueries
webSearchQueries.description: Web search queries for the following-up web search.

googleMapsWidgetContextToken.title: Googlemapswidgetcontexttoken
googleMapsWidgetContextToken.description: Optional. Output only. A token that can be used to render a Google Maps widget with the contextual data. This field is populated only when the grounding source is Google Maps. This field is not supported in Gemini API.

retrievalQueries.title: Retrievalqueries
retrievalQueries.description: Optional. The queries that were executed by the retrieval tools. This field is populated only when the grounding source is a retrieval tool, such as Vertex AI Search. This field is not supported in Gemini API.

sourceFlaggingUris.title: Sourceflagginguris
sourceFlaggingUris.description: Optional. Output only. A list of URIs that can be used to flag a place or review for inappropriate content. This field is populated only when the grounding source is Google Maps. This field is not supported in Gemini API.

GroundingMetadata-Input.title: GroundingMetadata
GroundingMetadata-Input.description: Information for various kinds of grounding.

imageSearchQueries.title: Imagesearchqueries
imageSearchQueries.description:
Optional. The image search queries that were used to generate the
      content. This field is populated only when the grounding source is Google
      Search with the Image Search search_type enabled.

groundingChunks.title: Groundingchunks
groundingChunks.description:
A list of supporting references retrieved from the grounding
      source. This field is populated when the grounding source is Google
      Search, Vertex AI Search, or Google Maps.

retrievalMetadata.description: Metadata related to retrieval in the grounding flow.

searchEntryPoint.description:
Optional. Google search entry for the following-up web
      searches.

webSearchQueries.title: Websearchqueries
webSearchQueries.description: Web search queries for the following-up web search.

googleMapsWidgetContextToken.title: Googlemapswidgetcontexttoken
googleMapsWidgetContextToken.description: Optional. Output only. A token that can be used to render a Google Maps widget with the contextual data. This field is populated only when the grounding source is Google Maps. This field is not supported in Gemini API.

retrievalQueries.title: Retrievalqueries
retrievalQueries.description: Optional. The queries that were executed by the retrieval tools. This field is populated only when the grounding source is a retrieval tool, such as Vertex AI Search. This field is not supported in Gemini API.

sourceFlaggingUris.title: Sourceflagginguris
sourceFlaggingUris.description: Optional. Output only. A list of URIs that can be used to flag a place or review for inappropriate content. This field is populated only when the grounding source is Google Maps. This field is not supported in Gemini API.

GroundingMetadata-Output.title: GroundingMetadata
GroundingMetadata-Output.description: Information for various kinds of grounding.

flagContentUri.title: Flagcontenturi
flagContentUri.description: The URI that can be used to flag the content.

sourceId.title: Sourceid
sourceId.description: The ID of the place or review.

GroundingMetadataSourceFlaggingUri.title: GroundingMetadataSourceFlaggingUri
GroundingMetadataSourceFlaggingUri.description:
A URI that can be used to flag a place or review for inappropriate content.

This is populated only when the grounding source is Google Maps. This data
type is not supported in Gemini API.

confidenceScores.title: Confidencescores
confidenceScores.description:
Confidence score of the support references.

      Ranges from 0 to 1. 1 is the most confident. This list must have the
      same size as the grounding_chunk_indices.

groundingChunkIndices.title: Groundingchunkindices
groundingChunkIndices.description:
A list of indices (into 'grounding_chunk') specifying the
      citations associated with the claim. For instance [1,3,4] means that
      grounding_chunk[1], grounding_chunk[3], grounding_chunk[4] are the
      retrieved content attributed to the claim.

segment.description: Segment of the content this support belongs to.

HttpAuth.title: HttpAuth
HttpAuth.description: The credentials and metadata for HTTP authentication.

HttpCredentials.title: HttpCredentials
HttpCredentials.description: Represents the secret token value for HTTP authentication, like user name, password, oauth token, etc.

IntermediateData-Input.title: IntermediateData
IntermediateData-Input.description: Container for intermediate data that an agent would generate as it responds with a final answer.

IntermediateData-Output.title: IntermediateData
IntermediateData-Output.description: Container for intermediate data that an agent would generate as it responds with a final answer.

minValue.title: Minvalue
minValue.description: The smaller end of the interval.

openAtMin.title: Openatmin
openAtMin.description: The interval is Open on the min end. The default value is False, which means that we assume that the interval is Closed.

maxValue.title: Maxvalue
maxValue.description: The larger end of the interval.

openAtMax.title: Openatmax
openAtMax.description: The interval is Open on the max end. The default value is False, which means that we assume that the interval is Closed.

Interval.title: Interval
Interval.description: Represents a range of numeric values, e.g. [0 ,1] or (2,3) or [-1, 6).

InvocationEvent-Input.title: InvocationEvent
InvocationEvent-Input.description:
An immutable record representing a specific point in the agent's invocation.

It captures agent's replies, requests to use tools (function calls), and tool
results.

This structure is a simple projection of the actual `Event` datamodel that
is intended for the Eval System.

InvocationEvent-Output.title: InvocationEvent
InvocationEvent-Output.description:
An immutable record representing a specific point in the agent's invocation.

It captures agent's replies, requests to use tools (function calls), and tool
results.

This structure is a simple projection of the actual `Event` datamodel that
is intended for the Eval System.

InvocationEvents-Input.title: InvocationEvents
InvocationEvents-Input.description: A container for events that occur during the course of an invocation.

InvocationEvents-Output.title: InvocationEvents
InvocationEvents-Output.description: A container for events that occur during the course of an invocation.

newHandle.title: Newhandle
newHandle.description: New handle that represents state that can be resumed. Empty if `resumable`=false.

resumable.title: Resumable
resumable.description: True if session can be resumed at this point. It might be not possible to resume session at some points. In that case we send update empty new_handle and resumable=false. Example of such case could be model executing function calls or just generating. Resuming session (using previous session token) in such state will result in some data loss.

lastConsumedClientMessageIndex.title: Lastconsumedclientmessageindex
lastConsumedClientMessageIndex.description:
Index of last message sent by client that is included in state represented by this SessionResumptionToken. Only sent when `SessionResumptionConfig.transparent` is set.

Presence of this index allows users to transparently reconnect and avoid issue of losing some part of realtime audio input/video. If client wishes to temporarily disconnect (for example as result of receiving GoAway) they can do it without losing state by buffering messages sent since last `SessionResmumptionTokenUpdate`. This field will enable them to limit buffering (avoid keeping all requests in RAM).

Note: This should not be used for when resuming a session at some time later -- in those cases partial audio and video frames arelikely not needed.

LiveServerSessionResumptionUpdate.title: LiveServerSessionResumptionUpdate
LiveServerSessionResumptionUpdate.description:
Update of the session resumption state.

Only sent if `session_resumption` was set in the connection config.

chosenCandidates.title: Chosencandidates
chosenCandidates.description: A list of the chosen candidate tokens at each decoding step. The length of this list is equal to the total number of decoding steps. Note that the chosen candidate might not be in `top_candidates`.

topCandidates.title: Topcandidates
topCandidates.description: A list of the top candidate tokens at each decoding step. The length of this list is equal to the total number of decoding steps.

LogprobsResult-Input.title: LogprobsResult
LogprobsResult-Input.description:
The log probabilities of the tokens generated by the model.

This is useful for understanding the model's confidence in its predictions and
for debugging. For example, you can use log probabilities to identify when the
model is making a less confident prediction or to explore alternative
responses that the model considered. A low log probability can also indicate
that the model is "hallucinating" or generating factually incorrect
information.

chosenCandidates.title: Chosencandidates
chosenCandidates.description: A list of the chosen candidate tokens at each decoding step. The length of this list is equal to the total number of decoding steps. Note that the chosen candidate might not be in `top_candidates`.

topCandidates.title: Topcandidates
topCandidates.description: A list of the top candidate tokens at each decoding step. The length of this list is equal to the total number of decoding steps.

LogprobsResult-Output.title: LogprobsResult
LogprobsResult-Output.description:
The log probabilities of the tokens generated by the model.

This is useful for understanding the model's confidence in its predictions and
for debugging. For example, you can use log probabilities to identify when the
model is making a less confident prediction or to explore alternative
responses that the model considered. A low log probability can also indicate
that the model is "hallucinating" or generating factually incorrect
information.

logProbability.title: Logprobability
logProbability.description: The log probability of this token. A higher value indicates that the model was more confident in this token. The log probability can be used to assess the relative likelihood of different tokens and to identify when the model is uncertain.

tokenId.title: Tokenid
tokenId.description: The token's numerical ID. While the `token` field provides the string representation of the token, the `token_id` is the numerical representation that the model uses internally. This can be useful for developers who want to build custom logic based on the model's vocabulary.

LogprobsResultCandidate.title: LogprobsResultCandidate
LogprobsResultCandidate.description: A single token and its associated log probability.

candidates.title: Candidates
candidates.description: The list of candidate tokens, sorted by log probability in descending order.

LogprobsResultTopCandidates.title: LogprobsResultTopCandidates
LogprobsResultTopCandidates.description:
A list of the top candidate tokens and their log probabilities at each decoding step.

This can be used to see what other tokens the model considered.

description.title: Description
description.description: A 2 to 3 line description of the metric.

metricValueInfo.description: Information on the nature of values supported by the metric.

MetricInfo.title: MetricInfo
MetricInfo.description: Information about the metric that are used for Evals.

interval.description: The values represented by the metric are of type interval.

MetricValueInfo.title: MetricValueInfo
MetricValueInfo.description: Information about the type of metric value.

modality.description: The modality associated with this token count.

tokenCount.title: Tokencount
tokenCount.description: The number of tokens counted for this modality.

ModalityTokenCount.title: ModalityTokenCount
ModalityTokenCount.description: Represents token counting info for a single modality.

OAuth2Auth.title: OAuth2Auth
OAuth2Auth.description: Represents credential value and its metadata for a OAuth2 credential.

mediaResolution.description: Media resolution for the input media.

codeExecutionResult.description: Optional. The result of executing the ExecutableCode.

executableCode.description: Optional. Code generated by the model that is intended to be executed.

fileData.description: Optional. The URI-based data of the part. This can be used to include files from Google Cloud Storage.

functionCall.description: Optional. A predicted function call returned from the model. This contains the name of the function to call and the arguments to pass to the function.

functionResponse.description: Optional. The result of a function call. This is used to provide the model with the result of a function call that it predicted.

inlineData.description: Optional. The inline data content of the part. This can be used to include images, audio, or video in a request.

text.title: Text
text.description: Optional. The text content of the part. When sent from the VSCode Gemini Code Assist extension, references to @mentioned items will be converted to markdown boldface text. For example `@my-repo` will be converted to and sent as `**my-repo**` by the IDE agent.

thought.title: Thought
thought.description: Optional. Indicates whether the `part` represents the model's thought process or reasoning.

thoughtSignature.title: Thoughtsignature
thoughtSignature.description: Optional. An opaque signature for the thought so it can be reused in subsequent requests.

videoMetadata.description: Optional. Video metadata. The metadata should only be specified while the video data is presented in inline_data or file_data.

toolCall.description: Server-side tool call. This field is populated when the model predicts a tool invocation that should be executed on the server. The client is expected to echo this message back to the API.

toolResponse.description: The output from a server-side ToolCall execution. This field is populated by the client with the results of executing the corresponding ToolCall.

Part-Input.title: Part
Part-Input.description:
A datatype containing media content.

Exactly one field within a Part should be set, representing the specific type
of content being conveyed. Using multiple fields within the same `Part`
instance is considered invalid.

mediaResolution.description: Media resolution for the input media.

codeExecutionResult.description: Optional. The result of executing the ExecutableCode.

executableCode.description: Optional. Code generated by the model that is intended to be executed.

fileData.description: Optional. The URI-based data of the part. This can be used to include files from Google Cloud Storage.

functionCall.description: Optional. A predicted function call returned from the model. This contains the name of the function to call and the arguments to pass to the function.

functionResponse.description: Optional. The result of a function call. This is used to provide the model with the result of a function call that it predicted.

inlineData.description: Optional. The inline data content of the part. This can be used to include images, audio, or video in a request.

text.title: Text
text.description: Optional. The text content of the part. When sent from the VSCode Gemini Code Assist extension, references to @mentioned items will be converted to markdown boldface text. For example `@my-repo` will be converted to and sent as `**my-repo**` by the IDE agent.

thought.title: Thought
thought.description: Optional. Indicates whether the `part` represents the model's thought process or reasoning.

thoughtSignature.title: Thoughtsignature
thoughtSignature.description: Optional. An opaque signature for the thought so it can be reused in subsequent requests.

videoMetadata.description: Optional. Video metadata. The metadata should only be specified while the video data is presented in inline_data or file_data.

toolCall.description: Server-side tool call. This field is populated when the model predicts a tool invocation that should be executed on the server. The client is expected to echo this message back to the API.

toolResponse.description: The output from a server-side ToolCall execution. This field is populated by the client with the results of executing the corresponding ToolCall.

Part-Output.title: Part
Part-Output.description:
A datatype containing media content.

Exactly one field within a Part should be set, representing the specific type
of content being conveyed. Using multiple fields within the same `Part`
instance is considered invalid.

level.description: The tokenization quality used for given media.

numTokens.title: Numtokens
numTokens.description: Specifies the required sequence length for media tokenization.

PartMediaResolution.title: PartMediaResolution
PartMediaResolution.description: Media resolution for the input media.

PartMediaResolutionLevel.title: PartMediaResolutionLevel
PartMediaResolutionLevel.description: The tokenization quality used for given media.

jsonPath.title: Jsonpath
jsonPath.description: Required. A JSON Path (RFC 9535) to the argument being streamed. https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc9535. e.g. "$.foo.bar[0].data".

willContinue.title: Willcontinue
willContinue.description: Optional. Whether this is not the last part of the same json_path. If true, another PartialArg message for the current json_path is expected to follow.

PartialArg.title: PartialArg
PartialArg.description:
Partial argument value of the function call.

This data type is not supported in Gemini API.

pageSpan.description: If populated, represents where the chunk starts and ends in the document.

RagChunk.title: RagChunk
RagChunk.description:
A RagChunk includes the content of a chunk of a RagFile, and associated metadata.

This data type is not supported in Gemini API.

firstPage.title: Firstpage
firstPage.description: Page where chunk starts in the document. Inclusive. 1-indexed.

lastPage.title: Lastpage
lastPage.description: Page where chunk ends in the document. Inclusive. 1-indexed.

RagChunkPageSpan.title: RagChunkPageSpan
RagChunkPageSpan.description:
Represents where the chunk starts and ends in the document.

This data type is not supported in Gemini API.

googleSearchDynamicRetrievalScore.title: Googlesearchdynamicretrievalscore
googleSearchDynamicRetrievalScore.description:
Optional. Score indicating how likely information from google
      search could help answer the prompt. The score is in the range [0, 1],
      where 0 is the least likely and 1 is the most likely. This score is only
      populated when google search grounding and dynamic retrieval is enabled.
      It will be compared to the threshold to determine whether to trigger
      Google search.

RetrievalMetadata.title: RetrievalMetadata
RetrievalMetadata.description: Metadata returned to client when grounding is enabled.

rubricContent.description: The actual testable criterion for the rubric.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the rubric that provide details on how the results of the rubric assessment be interpreted.

type.title: Type
type.description:
Optional. A type designator for the rubric, which can
      inform how it's evaluated or interpreted by systems or users.

      It's recommended to use consistent, well-defined, upper snake_case
      strings.

      Examples: "TOOL_USE_QUALITY", "FINAL_RESPONSE_QUALITY",
      "INSTRUCTION_ADHERENCE".

Rubric.title: Rubric
Rubric.description: This class represents a single Rubric.

textProperty.title: Textproperty
textProperty.description: The property being evaluated. Example: "The agent's response is grammatically correct."

rubricId.title: Rubricid
rubricId.description: The id of the rubric that was assessed.

score.title: Score
score.description: Score obtained after assessing the rubric. Optional, as assessment might not have happened.

RubricScore.title: RubricScore
RubricScore.description: The score obtained after applying the rubric to the Agent's response.

evalIds.title: Evalids
evalIds.description: This field is deprecated, use eval_case_ids instead.

evalCaseIds.title: Evalcaseids
evalCaseIds.description: List of eval case ids to evaluate. if empty, then all eval cases in the eval set are run.

evalMetricResults.title: Evalmetricresults
evalMetricResults.description: This field is deprecated, use overall_eval_metric_results instead.

customMetadata.title: Custommetadata
customMetadata.description: Optional metadata to associate with the artifact version.

SaveArtifactRequest.title: SaveArtifactRequest
SaveArtifactRequest.description: Request payload for saving a new artifact.

renderedContent.title: Renderedcontent
renderedContent.description:
Optional. Web content snippet that can be embedded in a web page
      or an app webview.

sdkBlob.title: Sdkblob
sdkBlob.description: Optional. JSON representing array of tuples.

SearchEntryPoint.title: SearchEntryPoint
SearchEntryPoint.description: The entry point used to search for grounding sources.

startIndex.title: Startindex
startIndex.description:
Output only. Start index in the given Part, measured in bytes.

      Offset from the start of the Part, inclusive, starting at zero.

endIndex.title: Endindex
endIndex.description:
Output only. End index in the given Part, measured in bytes.

      Offset from the start of the Part, exclusive, starting at zero.

partIndex.title: Partindex
partIndex.description:
Output only. The index of a Part object within its parent
      Content object.

text.title: Text
text.description:
Output only. The text corresponding to the segment from the
      response.

Segment.title: Segment
Segment.description: Segment of the content this support belongs to.

ServiceAccount.title: ServiceAccount
ServiceAccount.description:
Represents Google Service Account configuration.

Attributes:
  service_account_credential: The service account credential (JSON key).
  scopes: The OAuth2 scopes to request. Optional; when omitted with
      ``use_default_credential=True``, defaults to the cloud-platform scope.
  use_default_credential: Whether to use Application Default Credentials.
  use_id_token: Whether to exchange for an ID token instead of an access
      token. Required for service-to-service authentication with Cloud Run,
      Cloud Functions, and other Google Cloud services that require identity
      verification. When True, ``audience`` must also be set.
  audience: The target audience for the ID token, typically the URL of the
      receiving service (e.g. ``https://my-service-xyz.run.app``). Required
      when ``use_id_token`` is True.

ServiceAccountCredential.title: ServiceAccountCredential
ServiceAccountCredential.description:
Represents Google Service Account configuration.

Attributes:
  type: The type should be "service_account".
  project_id: The project ID.
  private_key_id: The ID of the private key.
  private_key: The private key.
  client_email: The client email.
  client_id: The client ID.
  auth_uri: The authorization URI.
  token_uri: The token URI.
  auth_provider_x509_cert_url: URL for auth provider's X.509 cert.
  client_x509_cert_url: URL for the client's X.509 cert.
  universe_domain: The universe domain.

Example:

    config = ServiceAccountCredential(
        type_="service_account",
        project_id="your_project_id",
        private_key_id="your_private_key_id",
        private_key="-----BEGIN PRIVATE KEY-----...",
        client_email="...@....iam.gserviceaccount.com",
        client_id="your_client_id",
        auth_uri="https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/auth",
        token_uri="https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token",
        auth_provider_x509_cert_url="https://www.googleapis.com/oauth2/v1/certs",
        client_x509_cert_url="https://www.googleapis.com/robot/v1/metadata/x509/...",
        universe_domain="googleapis.com"
    )


    config = ServiceAccountConfig.model_construct(**{
        ...service account config dict
    })

Session.title: Session
Session.description: Represents a series of interactions between a user and agents.

SessionInput.title: SessionInput
SessionInput.description: Values that help initialize a Session.

id.title: Id
id.description: Unique identifier of the tool call. The server returns the tool response with the matching `id`.

toolType.description: The type of tool that was called.

ToolCall.title: ToolCall
ToolCall.description:
A predicted server-side `ToolCall` returned from the model.

This message contains information about a tool that the model wants to invoke.
The client is NOT expected to execute this `ToolCall`. Instead, the
client should pass this `ToolCall` back to the API in a subsequent turn
within a `Content` message, along with the corresponding `ToolResponse`.

id.title: Id
id.description: The identifier of the tool call this response is for.

toolType.description: The type of tool that was called, matching the tool_type in the corresponding ToolCall.

ToolResponse.title: ToolResponse
ToolResponse.description:
The output from a server-side `ToolCall` execution.

This message contains the results of a tool invocation that was initiated by a
`ToolCall` from the model. The client should pass this `ToolResponse` back to
the API in a subsequent turn within a `Content` message, along with the
corresponding `ToolCall`.

ToolType.title: ToolType
ToolType.description: The type of tool in the function call.

TrafficType.title: TrafficType
TrafficType.description:
Output only.

The traffic type for this request. This enum is not supported in Gemini API.

finished.title: Finished
finished.description: The bool indicates the end of the transcription.

UiWidget.title: UiWidget
UiWidget.description:
Rendering metadata for a UI widget associated with an event.

When present on an Event.actions, the UI renders the widget using the
specified provider's renderer component.

UpdateMemoryRequest.title: UpdateMemoryRequest
UpdateMemoryRequest.description: Request to add a session to the memory service.

UpdateSessionRequest.title: UpdateSessionRequest
UpdateSessionRequest.description: Request to update session state without running the agent.

description.title: Description
description.description: General description of the expected behavior. This will be used in bot the instructions for the user simulator and the user simulator evaluator.

behavior_instructions.title: Behavior Instructions
behavior_instructions.description: Instructions the user should follow. These will be included in the instructions for the user simulator.

violation_rubrics.title: Violation Rubrics
violation_rubrics.description: Rubrics to evaluate whether the user simulator presents the behavior. If the user response presents any of these violations, the evaluator will consider the user simulator response as invalid.

UserBehavior.title: UserBehavior
UserBehavior.description: Container for the behavior of a persona.

id.title: Id
id.description: Human readable identifier for the UserPersona. Persona registries will refer to this identifier.

description.title: Description
description.description: Description for the UserPersona. This will be included in the instructions for the user simulator and its verifier.

behaviors.title: Behaviors
behaviors.description: Sequence of UserBehaviors for the persona. These will be included in the instructions for the user simulator and its verifier.

endOffset.title: Endoffset
endOffset.description: Optional. The end offset of the video.

fps.title: Fps
fps.description: Optional. The frame rate of the video sent to the model. If not specified, the default value is 1.0. The valid range is (0.0, 24.0].

startOffset.title: Startoffset
startOffset.description: Optional. The start offset of the video.

VideoMetadata.title: VideoMetadata
VideoMetadata.description: Provides metadata for a video, including the start and end offsets for clipping and the frame rate.
